examples: L23: joined Jaina's field-model explanation into a built sentence so she sounds analytical rather than clipped-by-default.
L29: changed the last-step explanation to a cleaner Jaina cadence with a semicolon instead of a hard snap.
L33-L41: smoothed the professional-recognition passage so the disgust and admiration live in one mind, not in stop-start notes.
L97-L111: reshaped Arthas's entrance into remembered cadence instead of stacked declaratives, keeping the old intimacy under control.
L227-L243: trimmed trader-description over-explanation so the road-man voice stays practical and forgettable except for the farewell.
L53-L57: kept Sylvanas's patience as Jaina's read of her posture, not a fact claim about Sylvanas's interior state.
L121-L123: Arthas's briefing read stays on what Jaina can watch him do with papers and roads, not on any hidden motive beyond her history with him.
L149-L151: cut the heavier narrator gloss after the refusal so the scene lands through Jaina's judgment, not editorial explanation.
L243-L245: the repeated-farewell clue now stays explicitly secondhand for Jaina; she remembers Sylvanas's account, not an event she witnessed.
L297-L307: Andorhal's reveal remains body-filtered through Jaina's wards and pain response instead of pulling back to omniscient city description.

notes: The opening keeps the chapter5 handoff intact: same four riders, same southwest line, same urgency about reaching Andorhal.
Jaina remains correctly incomplete on the final undeath step; she isolates ingestion, body participation, and death-lock without solving the whole mechanism.
Arthas's refusal reads as visible-war priority under rank pressure, not as simple personal cruelty, matching the plan constraint.
The old grain-seller clue remains unresolved pattern only; no false recognition, no Mal'Ganis implication, no proof inflation.
Andorhal is treated as the already-established source-node target; the surprise is the city's false normalcy, not the fact that the roads converge there.

examples: Opening analysis pass: reduced telegram chaining in Jaina's grain-body-death model by adding connective tissue and one longer sentence wave.
Kirin Tor reflection: folded the banned-questions material into a single flowing paragraph so the thought sounds accumulated, not listed.
Arthas entrance: merged several short beats into longer memory-shaped sentences while keeping the scene brisk.
Post-briefing exchange with Sylvanas: tightened short-line drift without flattening the cold professional rhythm between them.
Andorhal reveal: changed the horizon description from stacked short clauses into broader descriptive sweeps that match the scale of the city.
Chapter 5 -> 6 opening: the road scene now carries the prior chapter's dawn departure and route choice without any reset.
Scene 2 aftermath: Arthas's refusal carries directly into Jaina and Sylvanas's operational read instead of dropping the sting between scenes.
Trader clue -> city approach: the repeated farewell stays in the air long enough to bridge into the worsening contamination field.
The Strahnbrad stop still functions as the price of reaching Andorhal by the survivable line, preserving the chapterBridge logic.
The Andorhal close lands on the question the next chapter needs: a working city at the center of the plague roads.
L41-L45: reduced explanatory scaffolding around Jaina's revulsion so the bread image does the work without extra annotation.
L149-L151: removed a layer of post-Arthas explanation that repeated what the scene had already shown.
L227-L243: cut trader-description throat-clearing so the clue arrives through the farewell, not through narrator insistence.
L297-L307: turned the Andorhal arrival into fewer, stronger paragraph beats and avoided repetitive fragment stacking.
Scene openings were pressure-tested for cliché; all four major openings begin in motion, perception, or active briefing pressure rather than weather or waking.
L175-L179: Sylvanas's read of Arthas changed from report-ish 'healer's language / sorted it with the physicians' to plainer spoken language.
Jaina's plague speech was checked against the zero-tolerance list; no vector/actionable/ingested-style jargon survives in dialogue.
Arthas's refusal stays command-spoken and specific to riders, villages, and swords rather than drifting into memo language.
The trader keeps roadman's diction—route markers, bushel price, damp grain—without sounding marked or sinister too early.
No quoted line uses institutional or clinical offenders from the slop hitlist's dialogue-register section.

textureMetrics.note: Texture improved sharply, but participial usage remains just below the 5.0 floor. If the auditor enforces the floor strictly, this chapter is the likely candidate for a local texture-revision pass rather than a full craft rewrite.

researchChecks: Strahnbrad kept as a muddy military choke-point in the Alterac/Hillsbrad corridor.
Arthas calibrated as front-line paladin-prince committed to the visible emergency.
Andorhal kept logistical first: granary towers, loading yards, flour sweetness, open gates, active traffic.
Jaina remains pre-Theramore: exact, quick, humane, and not yet later-era bitter.
Thalassian horses continue to read as endurance-bred road mounts that tolerate magical hum until the field becomes truly wrong.
